# Cara

> Cara is animal shelter management software built for Irish and European rescue organisations. It also operates carashelters.ie/adopt — a public directory of animals available for adoption from shelters across Ireland.

Cara helps rescue shelters replace spreadsheets and Facebook DMs with a purpose-built platform. Shelters manage animals, adoption applications, digital contracts, donor records, and public adoption portals in one place.

## Key pages

- [Home](https://carashelters.ie): Overview of Cara shelter management software for Irish rescues
- [Adopt](https://carashelters.ie/adopt): Browse all animals available for adoption across shelters in Ireland — dogs, cats, rabbits and more
- [About](https://carashelters.ie/about): Cara's mission and background
- [Register](https://carashelters.ie/register): Register an animal rescue shelter on Cara

## What carashelters.ie/adopt contains

The /adopt page lists every animal currently marked as available for adoption across all shelters registered on Cara. Each listing shows:
- Animal name, species, and breed
- Approximate age and sex
- The shelter's name and county in Ireland
- A direct link to apply to adopt that animal

Users can filter by species (dog, cat, rabbit, bird, small animal, farm animal, reptile) and by county.

## What Cara does for shelters

- Animal intake records with photos, medical history, and current status
- Online adoption application forms — no paper required
- Digital e-sign adoption contracts sent by email
- A public-facing adoption portal for each shelter (e.g. carashelters.ie/portal/shelter-name)
- Team management with admin and staff roles
- Donor management and one-off donation collection via Stripe
- GDPR tools for handling data requests

## Pricing

Cara charges €34.99 per month per shelter, with a 30-day free trial. There is no setup fee.

## About Cara

Cara is designed specifically for Irish and European animal rescue charities and shelters. It is built to replace the informal systems — spreadsheets, Facebook groups, paper contracts — that many small rescues still rely on. The goal is to help more animals find homes faster by making the adoption process simple for both shelters and adopters.

Cara is based in Ireland.
